description: The gull had a white head with a black, heavy, vertical slash mark behind the eye. Another back, thinner (and smudgy) line was directly above the eye. The bill was black in color, except for a very small dull orange tip. (I thought that the base of the bill may have been slightly orangish also.)  The upperparts were almost entirely dark gray. (A white scapular crescent and white-tipped secondaries contrasted with the gray color.) The folded wing on the resting bird was black with several white apical spots visible. The underparts were white. The legs were dark red. In flight, I noticed the underwing coverts were all light in color. The underside of the primaries appeared darker with black wing tips.  The underside of the secondaries were darker than the underwing coverts.  I estimated that the gull was about 50% longer than a Killdeer that stood beside the gull briefly.
